findings.finding: Neither function passes category= when constructing MemoryEmbedding(...). The ORM column defaults to category="task" (db/models.py:459-461), so every architecture-note and failure row is silently mistagged as category="task" regardless of its real outcome.
findings.evidence: store.py:203-211 (embed_architecture_note, outcome="architecture", no category=); store.py:296-304 (embed_failure, outcome="failure", no category=); db/models.py:459-461 (category default="task"). Migration 010's own stated purpose was exactly this category tagging.
findings.production_impact: GET /api/memory/patterns?category=architecture and ?category=failure return zero rows even when matching data exists -- the feature this column was added for is broken for 2 of its 4 categories. categoryDistribution aggregate is correspondingly wrong.
findings.recommendation: Add category="architecture" / category="failure" to the two MemoryEmbedding(...) calls, matching embed_learning_signal's existing correct pattern; backfill existing mistagged rows.
findings.fix_note: Added category= to both MemoryEmbedding(...) calls. Added migration 021 to backfill existing mistagged rows. Live-verified against real dev DB.

findings.finding: Creates indexes on lesson_id/topic/state but none on the embedding column, unlike memory_embeddings (migration 004), which has an explicit HNSW index.
findings.production_impact: _find_most_similar_published() runs a full sequential-scan cosine-distance ORDER BY on every publish() call (every lesson extraction fleet-wide when VOYAGE_API_KEY is set). Fine at current scale, degrades as versioned_lessons grows.
findings.recommendation: Add a migration creating an HNSW index on versioned_lessons.embedding, matching the memory_embeddings pattern.
findings.fix_note: Added migration 020 creating versioned_lessons_embedding_hnsw. Applied to the real dev DB and confirmed present via pg_indexes.

findings.finding: No entry for memory_embeddings. versioned_lessons has its own dedicated archive_expired() + background loop, but memory_embeddings (the oldest memory system) has never had any retention/archival policy, and lacks the archived/archived_at columns the other 3 retained tables gained in migration 019.
findings.evidence: retention.py:30-34 dict has exactly 3 keys (task_logs, agent_runs, artifacts); migration 019 does not touch memory_embeddings.
findings.production_impact: memory_embeddings grows unbounded -- every task/epic completion, architecture note, failure, and learning signal writes a permanent row with no cleanup path.
findings.recommendation: Add memory_embeddings to the retention policy (needs archived/archived_at columns first), or explicitly document unbounded retention as deliberate.
findings.fix_note: Added memory_embeddings_retention_days config field (own knob, not reusing LOG_RETENTION_DAYS, matching the lesson_retention_days precedent). Added migration 022 (archived/archived_at + index), updated ORM model, wired into retention.py's cleanup cycle. Applied to real dev DB and confirmed via information_schema.columns.

findings.finding: Zero real (non-test) callers anywhere in app/. The same 'built but never wired' pattern this module's publish()/archive_expired() were both previously found-and-fixed for, recurring here for a third function with no acknowledging comment.
findings.evidence: grep -rn "get_versioned_memory_store()\." app finds exactly 2 real call sites (.publish, .archive_expired); .rollback( appears nowhere as a real call.
findings.production_impact: Rollback capability exists and is tested in isolation but cannot currently be triggered by any real code path (no API/UI/scheduled caller).
findings.recommendation: Wire rollback() into a real trigger (e.g. a memory/fleet dashboard action), or document it as intentionally manual/operator-only tooling.
findings.fix_note: Added GET /api/memory/lessons (list/discover a lesson_id) and POST /api/memory/lessons/{lesson_id}/rollback (require_approver-gated, dispatches via asyncio.to_thread) to api/memory.py. Live end-to-end verified via real TestClient against the real dev DB: publish -> merge -> list -> unauthenticated rollback rejected -> authenticated rollback restores real prior content -> unknown lesson_id returns 404.

findings.finding: _merge_via_llm(...) uses settings.model_planner instead of settings.model_router (the field config.py itself documents as "Model for triage/summary/heartbeat"). Only resolves to the cheap tier today because model_planner's current default coincidentally equals model_router's default.
findings.evidence: versioned_memory.py:262; config.py model_planner default=claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 described as "Model for PM/Architect/Decomposer", vs model_router same default value described as "Model for triage/summary/heartbeat".
findings.production_impact: If model_planner is ever changed independently, every lesson-merge call fleet-wide silently starts using a more expensive model with no code signal of the cost shift.

findings.finding: Never reads state["memory_context"], unlike pm_node and architect_node which both append it via a memory_block. Only reads pm_brief and architect_plan.
findings.evidence: grep -n "memory_context" across pm.py/architect.py/decomposer.py: 2 hits, 2 hits, 0 hits respectively.
findings.production_impact: Low -- decomposer still receives upstream memory-informed artifacts (pm_brief/architect_plan), but the 'memory_context flows PM->Architect->Decomposer' claim is only true for 2 of 3 stages.
findings.recommendation: Add the same memory_block append to decomposer_node for consistency, or document the omission as deliberate.
findings.fix_note: Added the same memory_block append pattern (from pm.py/architect.py) to decomposer_node's prompt construction.

fix_verification.pytest: 2758 passed, 0 failed, 55 skipped, 17 deselected (3 pre-existing retention tests updated for the new memory_embeddings_retention_days settings field -- expected, intentional new behavior)
fix_verification.migrations_applied: 020_versioned_lessons_hnsw, 021_backfill_memory_category, 022_memory_embeddings_retention -- all applied to the real dev DB (alembic upgrade head) and independently confirmed via pg_indexes/information_schema.columns
fix_verification.live_verification: embed_architecture_note/embed_failure category tagging, and a full publish->merge->list->rollback(rejected/accepted)->404 cycle, all exercised against the real dev DB via a real TestClient
fix_verification.also_fixed_in_passing: LLM_CALL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (Audit 02 fix) was missing from .env.example; added it and the new MEMORY_EMBEDDINGS_RETENTION_DAYS field, re-ran a Settings-vs-.env.example diff, 0 fields missing

not_found: Embedding fallback crash on missing VOYAGE_API_KEY -- confirmed graceful (zero-vector return)
versioned_memory.publish() gating regression -- confirmed still gated on a real voyage_api_key
Cross-querying between memory_embeddings and versioned_lessons -- 0 found, each function queries only its own table
asyncio.run()-from-a-running-loop hazard -- confirmed NOT present; both publish() and archive_expired() are correctly isolated inside asyncio.to_thread() contexts
Cross-tenant memory leakage -- N/A, this system has no multi-tenant design anywhere; global memory scope is consistent with its actual single-tenant scope
Raw memory-write API reachable without the agent pipeline -- 0 found; api/memory.py exposes only 2 GET routes
N+1 query patterns in the 4 query_* functions -- 0 found
